summary: Backend testing of extended Portfolio feature (deep-case fields + inquiry CRUD + admin routing fix). 26/27 tests passed (96%). Routing fix VERIFIED: /api/admin/portfolio/inquiries (GET/PATCH/DELETE) no longer shadowed by /{case_id} and works correctly. Public list/detail/featured + enrichment of 5 seeded cases verified. Public inquiry creation across all 3 intents (order_similar, consultation, calculate) works including null case_id. Admin PATCH (status transitions new→contacted→qualified→converted→closed, invalid status → 400, internal_notes) and DELETE work. /api/admin/portfolio/{case_id} GET + PATCH (published, featured, title toggles) still works after reorder. Auth gating correct: public endpoints open, admin endpoints reject anon (401) and non-admin client (403). ONE FAILURE: POST /api/portfolio/inquiry silently coerces invalid intent to 'order_similar' instead of returning 400 as spec requires.

minor.issue: Invalid `intent` values are silently coerced to 'order_similar' instead of being rejected with HTTP 400. Spec explicitly requires rejection. Reference: server.py:6897-6899 (`if intent not in (...): intent = 'order_similar'`). Recommended fix: raise HTTPException(400, detail='Invalid intent') when intent is provided but not in the allowed set.

action_items: Fix /api/portfolio/inquiry intent validation in /app/backend/server.py around lines 6897-6899 — raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid intent') when payload['intent'] is provided but not in {'order_similar','consultation','calculate'} (currently silently coerced to 'order_similar').
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~27,878 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting per-domain (portfolio, money, payouts, validation, auth) into routers. Not part of this iteration's scope but flagging as long-standing tech debt.

POST /api/portfolio/inquiry uses `dict = Body(...)` instead of a Pydantic input model. Switching to a Pydantic `PortfolioInquiryCreate` model would catch invalid intents at validation time (422) for free and removes ~25 lines of manual validation.
PATCH /api/admin/portfolio/inquiries/{inquiry_id} validates status well (returns 400 on bad value) — mirrors the same approach that the POST /portfolio/inquiry endpoint is missing.

Routing fix is correct: the 3 /admin/portfolio/inquiries handlers are now defined ABOVE /admin/portfolio/{case_id} (server.py lines 6691, 6713, 6747 vs 6759). Comment at line 6687-6690 explains the precedence rationale — good practice.
seed_data_creation: Tests create several TEST_-prefixed portfolio inquiries (full_name starts with TEST_, emails like TEST_*@example.com). Tests are non-destructive on the 5 seeded portfolio_cases (PATCH toggles are reverted at the end). Test_delete_inquiry cleans up its own row.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use _login() helper in test_portfolio_extension.py to handle the Secure-flagged session_token cookie when testing over http://localhost:8001. The backend issues `SameSite=none; Secure` cookies which the `requests` library will NOT re-send over plain HTTP — you must extract the token from Set-Cookie and re-register without the Secure flag (or test via the https preview URL).
rca_of_issue: RCA for the single failing test (invalid intent not rejected): In /app/backend/server.py at line 6897-6899, the inquiry endpoint reads `intent = str(payload.get('intent','')).strip(); if intent not in ('order_similar','consultation','calculate'): intent = 'order_similar'`. This silently rewrites bad input. The review_request explicitly states 'POST /api/portfolio/inquiry — rejects invalid intent with 400'. Reproduction: `curl -X POST http://localhost:8001/api/portfolio/inquiry -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"intent":"bogus","full_name":"x","email":"a@b.com","message":"hi"}'` returns 200 with intent='order_similar' in the response. Mitigation: replace the silent coercion with `raise HTTPException(400, detail='Invalid intent')`.
